key_insight: The paper develops a four-field mixed formulation for Biot consolidation that conserves angular momentum and fluid mass pointwise using strongly conservative finite elements.
engineering_relevance: This enables more physically faithful and stable poromechanics simulations for soil, rock, and geotechnical consolidation problems.
method: Formulate Biot’s quasi-static consolidation with stress, displacement, flux, and pressure as unknowns.
Prove well-posedness in suitable Hilbert spaces, including a novel space for the displacement-flux pair.
Design H(div div)-conforming mixed-mixed finite elements that enforce strong local conservation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical validation through well-posedness proofs and optimal a priori error estimates for the discrete method.
watch_item: The approach relies on specialized H(div div)-conforming elements and a novel function-space setting, which may increase implementation complexity.
paper_title: A family of mixed-mixed strongly conservative finite element methods for Biot's model of consolidation
